headline: 8 contracts catalogued on Ethereum with confirmed proxy architecture; multi-chain deployment incomplete due to access restrictions
short_headline: Ethereum contracts identified; full multi-chain enumeration blocked

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search surfaced 3 CIAN Yield Layer token contracts on Ethereum: ylstETH (0xB13aa2d0345b0439b064f26B82D8dCf3f508775d), ylpumpBTC (0xd4cc9b31e9ef33e392ff2f81ad52be8523e0993b), ciETH (0xcdd374f491fbf3f4fcf6e9023c99043774005137)
Canonical deployed contracts documentation exists at https://docs.cian.app/ with reference to Yield Layer Contracts page, but access is restricted; DefiLlama tracks 8 pools across protocol
No audit links were provided in protocol context; CryptoBriefing references GitHub documentation but no audit PDFs found in search results

GitHub repo https://github.com/cian-ai/cian-protocol exists with basic structure showing Solidity contracts and OpenZeppelin dependencies; no deployment artifacts (broadcast/ or deployments/ folders) visible in brief repo scan

Multi-chain enumeration incomplete: protocol operates on Ethereum, Mantle, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, BSC, Scroll per DefiLlama, but only Ethereum addresses extracted due to website JavaScript requirement and docs access restrictions
No factory contracts identified yet; 8 pools tracked by DefiLlama may represent individual vault deployments but enumeration view not accessed

ylstETH contract 0xB13aa2d0345b0439b064f26B82D8dCf3f508775d confirmed as TransparentUpgradeableProxy with implementation 0x03a16e0Bfa2C5c28991c0446fC849211de5fa108 and admin 0x8FA9aa69a6e94c1cd49FbF214C833B2911D02553; actor_class unknown pending further investigation
All Ethereum addresses traced to Etherscan block-explorer URLs with verified source code; off-chain references (GitHub, docs, Medium) support but do not replace on-chain evidence

rationale.verdict: Discovery catalog spans Ethereum mainnet with 3+ vault tokens and proxy contracts identified via Etherscan. Multi-chain enumeration blocked by website JavaScript requirement and documentation access restrictions. GitHub repository confirms Solidity codebase but deployment artifacts not yet accessed. Full address book will require either direct docs access, explorer-by-explorer manual searches on remaining chains, or subsequent runs with inherited address hints.

evidence.shows: ylstETH token contract is a TransparentUpgradeableProxy deployed at 0xB13aa2d0345b0439b064f26B82D8dCf3f508775d with implementation 0x03a16e0Bfa2C5c28991c0446fC849211de5fa108; admin 0x8FA9aa69a6e94c1cd49FbF214C833B2911D02553; 40 holders; verified source code
ylpumpBTC (CIAN yield layer pumpBTC) ERC-20 token contract on Ethereum; 4 holders; 316.41 total supply
ciETH (CIAN ETH-stETH strategy pool) token contract showing TransparentUpgradeableProxy interface with admin() and implementation() functions
Source repository for CIAN protocol written in Solidity; dependencies on @openzeppelin/contracts 4.5.0 and @openzeppelin/contracts-upgradeable 4.5.2; 12 stars, 1 fork, minimal history (2 commits)

CIAN documentation hub references two products: Bondify and Yield Layer; Yield Layer Documentation links to separate GitBook space; mentions Yield Layer Contracts page with per-network address listings

DefiLlama tracker shows TVL $385.75M across 7 chains: Mantle $217.15M, Ethereum $166.57M, Arbitrum $2.02M, OP Mainnet $12,295, Base $1,346, BSC $1,002, Scroll $0; 8 pools tracked; references GitHub code links

CIAN architecture uses multi-signature wallet for asset allocation with multi-party consensus for fund allocation and parameter changes; strategy contracts receive allocated assets; founded 2022 by Luffy He

Documentation sitemap confirms existence of Yield Layer Contracts page at /yieldlayer/for-builders-developer-documentation/yield-layer-contracts.md containing all contract addresses organized by network

unknowns: D2: Deployed contracts documentation page (https://docs.cian.app/yieldlayer/for-builders-developer-documentation/yield-layer-contracts.md) exists but is access-restricted; cannot extract per-chain address list
D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (broadcast/ and deployments/ folders) not visible in brief repo navigation; full artifact search requires authenticated API or deeper repo traversal
D5: Mantle deployment addresses not enumerated (zero results from block-explorer search attempts; likely requires direct Mantle interface access)
D5: Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, BSC, Scroll deployment addresses not enumerated due to protocol website requiring JavaScript and no alternative on-chain enumeration attempted
D6: Factory contract addresses and child vault enumeration not attempted; 8 pools on DefiLlama may indicate factory pattern but requires ABIreads
D6-offchain: DefiLlama tracks 8 pools but enumeration method (factory vs. manual index) and per-chain breakdown unknown

D7: Actor class for multisig admin 0x8FA9aa69a6e94c1cd49FbF214C833B2911D02553 not yet determined (EOA, multisig threshold, timelock); Medium references 'multi-signature wallet' but signature structure not verified on-chain

protocol_metadata.about: CIAN is a yield aggregator that consolidates and redistributes yield sources across DeFi protocols for liquid staking derivatives and restaking tokens. Users deposit assets like stETH and receive yield-bearing vault tokens (ylstETH, ylpumpBTC) that automatically optimize returns through multiple integrated yield strategies and protocols. The protocol operates multisig-controlled vaults with transparent strategy contracts deployed across seven blockchain networks.
